encryption.title: VLESS encryption client configuration
encryption.description: (native/xorpub XTLS Vision supports Splice. 1-RTT only / 0-RTT reuse if the server-issued ticket lifetime is non-zero.) Pick one with /; at least one base64 entry follows and can be chained indefinitely, generated with mihomo generate vless-x25519 and mihomo generate vless-mlkem768. Drop the parentheses when substituting values. Padding is optional and applies only in 1-RTT mode to mask handshake length. Both ends default to 100-111-1111.75-0-111.50-0-3333: after the 1-RTT client/server hello, append 111-1111 bytes of random padding with 100% probability, then wait 0-111 ms with 75% probability (probability-from-to), then send 0-3333 bytes of random padding with 50% probability (no Write() if length is 0). Server and client may set different padding parameters; chain them indefinitely in len, gap order; the first padding entry must have 100% probability and ≥35 bytes.
encryption.markdownDescription: (native/xorpub XTLS Vision supports Splice. 1-RTT only / 0-RTT reuse if the server-issued ticket lifetime is non-zero.) Pick one with /; at least one base64 entry follows and can be chained indefinitely, generated with mihomo generate vless-x25519 and mihomo generate vless-mlkem768. Drop the parentheses when substituting values. Padding is optional and applies only in 1-RTT mode to mask handshake length. Both ends default to 100-111-1111.75-0-111.50-0-3333: after the 1-RTT client/server hello, append 111-1111 bytes of random padding with 100% probability, then wait 0-111 ms with 75% probability (probability-from-to), then send 0-3333 bytes of random padding with 50% probability (no Write() if length is 0). Server and client may set different padding parameters; chain them indefinitely in len, gap order; the first padding entry must have 100% probability and ≥35 bytes.

On Android: only local IPv4 connections are forwarded; to share your VPN connection via hotspot or relay, use VPNHotspot. On Linux: auto-redirect with auto-route now works as expected on routers without manual intervention.
auto-redirect.markdownDescription:
Automatically configure iptables/nftables to redirect TCP connections (requires auto-route enabled).

On Android: only local IPv4 connections are forwarded; to share your VPN connection via hotspot or relay, use [VPNHotspot](https://github.com/Mygod/VPNHotspot).

On Linux: `auto-redirect` with `auto-route` now works as expected on routers without manual intervention.

strict-route.title: Strict routing
strict-route.description: On Linux, prevents address leaks and makes DNS hijacking work on Android and Linux with systemd-resolved (your device will be unreachable from other devices). On Windows, adds a firewall rule to block DNS leaks caused by the Smart Multi-Homed Name Resolution behavior (may break some applications such as VirtualBox).
strict-route.markdownDescription: On Linux, prevents address leaks and makes DNS hijacking work on Android and Linux with `systemd-resolved` (your device will be unreachable from other devices). On Windows, adds a firewall rule to block DNS leaks caused by the `Smart Multi-Homed Name Resolution` behavior (may break some applications such as `VirtualBox`).
